item.title: The Prebiotic 2'-Fucosyllactose Ameliorates Colitis via an Akkermansia muciniphila-Mediated Prebiotic-Microbiota-Metabolite Axis.
item.description: Journal of agricultural and food chemistry - Recent studies indicate that 2'-fucosyllactose (2'-FL), a human milk oligosaccharide, alleviates inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) by modulating the gut microbiota, yet the underlying mechanisms and...

item.title: Clonal overlap and convergent clustering of T cell receptor signatures in Crohn's disease in monozygotic twins.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The dysregulated immune response in Crohn's disease might result from disturbances in the T cell receptor (TCR) repertoire. To investigate this hypothesis, we compared the peripheral TCR repertoire...

item.title: ACY-1215 ameliorates experimental colitis by inhibiting dendritic cell maturation and Th1/Th17 responses.

item.title: Engineering a Natural Ferritin Nanozyme-Probiotic Biosynthesis System for Inflammatory Bowel Disease Therapy.

item.title: Bifidobacterium breve207-1 protects against later-life colitis following early-life antibiotic exposure by enhancing intestinal barrier and immune homeostasis.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - The early colonization of bacteria has a significant impact on immune system development. The disruption of gut microbiota by antibiotics in early life is a major risk factor for pediatric inflamma...

item.title: Sophoraflavanone G alleviates ulcerative colitis in mice by reshaping the gut microbiome and restoring the Th17/Treg balance through the PI3K/AKT/NF-κB signaling pathway.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is characterized by diarrhea, bloody stool and a feeling of incomplete defecation. Sophoraflavanone G (SFG) is one of the main chemical components of Sophora flavescens Ait ...

item.title: Guggulsterone as a Modulator of the Microbiota-Bile Acid-FXR Axis: Molecular Mechanisms and Therapeutic Potential in Metabolic Ageing.
item.description: Biochimie - Review - Cholesterol biosynthesis and bile acid metabolism are tightly controlled by the Farnesoid X receptor (FXR), which integrates bile acid signaling with lipid, glucose, and inflammatory pathways to ma...

item.title: Antibiotic disruption of the gut microbiome triggers IBD-like proteolytic activity.
item.description: Cell reports - Antibiotics (Abx) are essential in medicine but can disrupt gut microbiota, potentially contributing to inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs). This study employed fecal metagenomics and metaproteomics...

item.title: Integrating host-microbiota metabolic networks: how aromatic amino acids shape immune homeostasis and affect disease progression.
item.description: Cellular & molecular immunology - Review - Gut microbial metabolism is intimately linked to host immune homeostasis. Aromatic amino acids (AAAs) serve as substrates for both host and microbial enzymes, yielding a diverse array of metabolite...

item.title: 1,2,4-trimethoxybenzene exerts multi-target effects against ulcerative colitis by inhibiting the NLRP3 inflammasome and remodeling the gut microbiota-metabolism axis.
item.description: European journal of pharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) with limited treatment options. The natural compound 1,2,4-Trimethoxybenzene (ZY12) is a known NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor, bu...

item.title: Host-targeted apple pectic oligosaccharides repair intestinal barrier in colitis via HSP90β/miRNA/CORO2A axis.

item.title: Pneumococcal Endopeptidase O Attenuates Colitis by Inhibiting the Macrophage-CCL2 Axis and Reshaping Gut Microbiota.

item.title: Microbiota-Sensitive Nanocarriers for the Targeted Delivery of Therapeutic Agents into the Gut: Advanced Treatment Strategy for Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
